title: libpod: compare graph driver DB override against user config, not resolved value
body:
The `mergeDBConfig` function corrects runtime config values to match those stored in the database. Every sibling override in this function already uses `Debugf`: run root, graph root, static dir, tmp dir, and volume path. Only the graph driver override was logged at `Errorf`, despite having identical semantics (the DB value unconditionally wins).

A config mismatch alone is not an error condition — it simply means the user's config or compiled-in default differs from the DB-stored driver. The DB is the authoritative source; the override is intentional.

This change is consistent with the existing `Debugf` pattern used for all other `mergeDBConfig` overrides on lines 1106, 1115, 1132, 1139, and 1146 of `libpod/runtime.go`.

comments.body: I would echo the question of what the problem we're trying to solve is - it doesn't seem to be clearly stated?

Closing — this wasn't solving a real bug. The current error correctly informs the user when the resolved driver differs from the database driver; that information is useful regardless of whether the source of the mismatch is a real misconfiguration or a vendor drop-in override.

For users hitting issue #29120: add your own drop-in at higher priority to override the distro default, e.g. ~/.config/containers/storage.conf.d/99-btrfs.conf with driver = "btrfs". This is by design per the v6 config file loading changes.

Thanks for the patience.
